concerns.text: Tasks T038–T049 (Reviewer Revisions) address simulated feedback from personas (Linus Pauling, Marie Curie) and introduce requirements (DFT-D4 comparison, lattice energy benchmarks, resonance energy discussion, geometry optimization) that are NOT present in spec.md FRs or USs. These tasks have no matching spec requirement, creating scope creep and untraceable work.

Task T042 ('Add section... discussing resonance-energy contributions...') has no corresponding FR/SC in spec.md. The spec does not require analysis of aromatic resonance contributions (≈35–40 kcal/mol) or their impact on binding.

Tasks T043/T044 (geometry optimization, bond lengths/angles) have no matching FR/SC in spec.md. The spec focuses on single-point energies and bulk property correlations, not geometry optimization or structural parameter reporting.

Tasks T039–T041 (DFT-D4 comparison, experimental lattice energy benchmarks) have no matching FR/SC in spec.md. The spec requires benchmarking against CCSD(T)/CBS references, not experimental lattice energies or DFT-D4 comparisons.

Tasks T045–T049 (uncertainty analysis, calibration procedure documentation referencing Grimme 2006) have no matching FR/SC in spec.md. While the spec mentions error metrics, it does not require explicit propagation of experimental uncertainties or a dedicated calibration methodology section.
Tasks in Phase 6 and 7 use labels [Rev-LP] and [Rev-MC] referencing simulated personas not defined in spec.md. This creates stale tags for non-existent requirements, violating traceability.
